body:
## Summary

Fixes #1662.

Telegram `/solve`-family commands now use the configured yargs parser to identify the first positional GitHub URL, so commands like this work:

```text
/codex --model gpt-5.4-mini https://github.com/konard/p-vs-np/issues/476
```

The fix also applies the same URL-position handling to `/hive`, while preserving reply-based URL extraction for commands such as `/solve --model opus`.

## Changes

- Added shared Telegram argument helpers for yargs parsing and URL-first normalization.
- Updated `/solve` and `/hive` URL validation to read yargs positional output instead of assuming `args[0]` is the URL.
- Kept malformed-flag validation before URL validation and after locked overrides are merged.
- Added `tests/test-telegram-options-before-url.mjs` and included it in `npm test`.
- Added a patch changeset.
- Added the requested case study and captured issue/PR artifacts under `docs/case-studies/issue-1662/`.

## Reproduction

Before the fix, `/codex --model gpt-5.4-mini <github-issue-url>` failed because `--model` was validated as the first argument.

After the fix, yargs identifies the issue URL as the command positional argument and the bot normalizes the internal execution args back to URL-first form.

## Verification

```bash
node tests/test-telegram-options-before-url.mjs
node tests/test-malformed-flags.mjs
npm run lint
npm test
```

## Evidence

Issue screenshot captured in the case study:

![Issue 1662 Telegram screenshot](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/issue-1662-f172a7412294/docs/case-studies/issue-1662/screenshot-08ce9e7a.png?raw=true)
